metadata.title: Tony Blair Institute / Ellison(Oracle) -> UK digital-ID -> Oracle infrastructure: the AI-core <-> digital-ID bridge
metadata.provenance: Sourced to Lighthouse Reports, New Statesman, Democracy for Sale, TBI's own reports, FOI'd minutes, FT/WaPo/Guardian, DatacenterDynamics/Register.
metadata.ai_core_linkage: Oracle is a CORE node of the AI circular SCC (data/graph.json). Its founder funds the think tank driving UK digital-ID policy, and Oracle is positioned to build that infrastructure. This is the most documented bridge between the AI-capex core and the digital-ID/age-verification push - alongside Altman/Worldcoin and Meta/age-verification-astroturf.

advocacy.position: Leading UK national-digital-ID advocate; 'Time for Digital ID' (Sep 2025) recommends a digital-ID superapp on GOV.UK One Login (claimed 62% support, ~GBP 2bn/yr benefit)
'A New National Purpose' - single digital ID for all residents + wallet + 'once only' principle
Recommended Tech Sec Peter Kyle consult the Ellison Institute (FOI'd 12 Sep 2024 meeting minutes)
Announced national digital-ID 'BritCard' (25 Sep 2025), initially mandatory for right-to-work; walked back compulsion to optional 14 Jan 2026
Firmly opposed ('used to control and penalize'); called the U-turn 'a victory for individual liberty'
2.9m+ signatures by 23 Oct 2025 against mandatory digital ID; TBI's 62% support figure reportedly collapsed to ~31% (FT leak)
Dominant UK-gov cloud incumbent (10yr ~$1bn across DWP/DEFRA/MoJ/Home Office; GBP 1bn+ public revenue since 2022); widely cited digital-ID infrastructure frontrunner (NO award confirmed)
Escalating age-assurance enforcement 2025-26 (GBP 1m Dec 2025; GBP 1.35m vs 8579 LLC Feb 2026; ~76 sites investigated) - tailwind normalizing online identity verification

grade_reasoning: Every link is documented: (1) Ellison Foundation gave/pledged ~£257m/~$348m to TBI since 2021; (2) TBI is the named leading UK digital-ID advocate; (3) Blair personally lobbied Tech Sec Peter Kyle to engage Ellison's institute (FOI'd minutes); (4) Oracle is the dominant UK-gov cloud incumbent; (5) Starmer adopted a digital-ID policy mirroring TBI's blueprint. Materially stronger than coincidental alignment. NOT closed: Oracle actually winning the digital-ID contract is positional/unconfirmed; funding-intent is inferred not proven; no illegality alleged. MODERATE leaning STRONG; upgrade to STRONG if an Oracle digital-ID award/bid is confirmed.
